meta.coverage: All 29 masters. Token/auto-layout checks sample each master's default variant.
meta.note: Facts captured from Figma via figma-console MCP read-tools. Rules live in check-figma.js.

components.name: Action/AtlButton
components.description:
Action button. Maps to AtlButtonSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: a user-initiated action — submit, save, delete, navigate. Pick the visual based on hierarchy: `primary` for the screen's main action (one per screen), `secondary` for supporting actions, `outline` for tertiary/inline, `danger` for destructive actions (delete, irreversible).

Don't use when: the action is a navigation link (use a real `<a>` element, not a styled button), the choice is between two states (use Form/AtlToggle), or the user is filling a form field (use Form/AtlInput / Form/AtlSelect).

API surface (Figma → spec):
- Variant `variant`: primary | secondary | outline | danger — maps to AtlButtonSpec.variant
- Variant `size`: sm | md | lg — maps to AtlButtonSpec.size
- Variant `state`: default | hover | focus | active — interaction state
- Boolean `disabled`: maps to AtlButtonSpec.disabled
- Boolean `loading`: maps to AtlButtonSpec.loading — shows spinner, disables interaction
- Boolean `hasIcon`: toggles the leading icon slot (Instance Swap → Icon library)

A11y: native HTML <button>. When the button has no visible label (icon-only), supply `aria-label` — required by spec. Disabled state uses HTML `disabled` (not aria-disabled) for buttons since they're never expected to remain in the tab order.

Status: structural restructure 2026-04-27 — `state` enum slimmed to interaction (default/hover/focus/active); `loading` and `disabled` moved out as Booleans matching AtlButtonSpec; `hasIcon` Boolean added for icon-slot toggle.

components.name: Form/AtlInput
components.description:
Single-line text input. Maps to AtlInputSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: a user needs to enter a short string (text, email, password, number, tel, url). For multi-line use Form/AtlTextarea. For options use Form/AtlSelect or Form/AtlCombobox.

Don't use when: input is binary (use Form/AtlCheckbox or Form/AtlToggle), the value is one-of-many (use Form/AtlRadioGroup), or the user is choosing from a set (use Form/AtlSelect).

API surface (Figma → spec):
- Variant `state`: default | hover | focus | invalid | filled — interaction + form state
- Boolean `disabled`: maps to AtlFormFieldSpec.disabled
- Boolean `readonly`: maps to AtlFormFieldSpec.readonly
- Boolean `required`: maps to AtlFormFieldSpec.required
- (code-only props on AtlInputSpec: `type`, `placeholder`, `value`, `onValueChange`, `name`)

A11y: native HTML <input>; ARIA roles and keyboard come from the browser. Disabled state uses aria-disabled (not HTML disabled) so screen readers still announce it — pair with a tooltip/helper text explaining why.

Status: structural restructure 2026-04-27 — old `state` enum (default/filled/focus/invalid/disabled/hover/active/loading) replaced by interaction Variant + orthogonal Booleans matching libs/spec.

components.name: Display/AtlCard
components.description:
Container for grouped content. Maps to AtlCardSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: distinct grouped content needs a visual container — list items, summaries, dashboard tiles. For overlay content use Overlay/AtlDialog or Overlay/AtlDrawer.

API surface:
- Variant `variant`: elevated | outlined | flat → AtlCardSpec.variant
- Variant `padding`: none | sm | md | lg → AtlCardSpec.padding
- Text `Title`, `Body`: content slots
- (code-only: `role` — landmark role, default plain div)

A11y: defaults to <div>. Set `role` only when the card is meaningful as a landmark (article, region) — adding a landmark for every card pollutes the page outline.

components.name: Display/AtlBadge
components.description:
Inline status pill. Maps to AtlBadgeSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: a small label needs to convey status, count, or category alongside other content. For larger callouts use Feedback/AtlAlert.

Don't use when: the content is a control (use Action/AtlButton) or it's the primary content of the screen.

API surface:
- Variant `variant`: default | success | warning | danger | info → AtlBadgeSpec.variant
- Variant `size`: sm | md → AtlBadgeSpec.size
- Text `Label`: visible label

A11y: badges are decorative by default. If they convey state changes important to assistive tech, wrap in role="status".

components.name: Form/AtlSelect
components.description:
Dropdown selector. Maps to AtlSelectSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: user picks one value from a fixed list of <50 options. For searchable lists use Form/AtlCombobox. For 2–7 visible options use Form/AtlRadioGroup. For multi-select use a Combobox or stacked Form/AtlCheckbox.

Don't use when: options are dynamic / fetched (use Form/AtlCombobox), the choice is binary (use Form/AtlToggle), or users benefit from seeing all options simultaneously (use Form/AtlRadioGroup).

API surface (Figma → spec):
- Variant `state`: default | hover | focus | open | filled
- Boolean `disabled`: maps to AtlFormFieldSpec.disabled
- Boolean `invalid`: maps to AtlFormFieldSpec.invalid
- Boolean `required`: maps to AtlFormFieldSpec.required
- Text `Placeholder`: maps to placeholder

A11y: native popover via the HTML Popover API. Keyboard: ArrowUp/Down navigate, Enter selects, Escape closes. Disabled state uses aria-disabled (not HTML disabled).

Status: structural restructure 2026-04-27 — `state` enum slimmed to interaction + open + filled; disabled/invalid/required moved to Booleans matching spec.

components.name: Overlay/AtlDialog
components.description:
Modal dialog. Maps to AtlDialogSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: user input is required before continuing (confirmation, form, error). For non-blocking notifications use Overlay/AtlToast; for side panels use Overlay/AtlDrawer.

API surface:
- Variant `size`: sm | md | lg | xl | full → AtlDialogSpec.size
- Text `Title`, `Message`, `ConfirmLabel`, `CancelLabel`: content slots
- (code-only: `open`, `closeOnBackdrop`, `onOpenChange`)

A11y: ARIA dialog pattern with focus trap. Esc closes; first focusable element receives focus on open.

components.name: Navigation/AtlTabGroup
components.description:
Tabbed content switcher. Maps to AtlTabGroupSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: peer content panels share a context and user picks one to view. For sequential steps use Navigation/AtlStepper.

API surface (Figma → spec):
- Variant `variant`: default | pills → AtlTabGroupSpec.variant
- Variant `selected`: 0 | 1 — illustrative selection state
- Variant `state`: default — interaction state (legacy mash-up removed)
- Boolean `disabled`: disables every contained Tab
- Boolean `loading`: shows tab-bar skeleton / panel spinner

A11y: ARIA tabs pattern. Arrow keys move selection inside the tablist; Tab moves out. aria-controls links each tab to its panel.

Status: 2026-04-27 — `state` Variant slimmed from {default,focus,hover,active,loading,disabled,error} to default-only; disabled/loading moved to Booleans matching the Form/* pattern.

components.name: Feedback/AtlAccordionGroup
components.description:
Collapsible content sections. Maps to AtlAccordionGroupSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: content is partitioned into sections that don't all need to be visible simultaneously (FAQ, settings groups). For peer content where one is always visible use Navigation/AtlTabGroup.

API surface:
- Variant `variant`: default | bordered | separated → AtlAccordionGroupSpec.variant
- (code-only: `multi` — boolean for allowing multiple panels open)

A11y: ARIA accordion pattern. Each panel's heading wraps a button; aria-expanded reflects state.

components.name: Navigation/AtlMenu
components.description:
Action menu. Maps to AtlMenuSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: secondary actions on an item, triggered by a control (button, more icon). For navigation use a sidebar or Navigation/AtlTabGroup.

API surface:
- Variant `variant`: default | compact → AtlMenuSpec.variant

A11y: ARIA menu pattern with roving tabindex; arrow keys navigate, Enter/Space activates, Escape closes.

components.name: Overlay/AtlTooltip
components.description:
Floating context label. Maps to AtlTooltipSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: brief non-essential clarification on hover/focus (e.g. icon-only buttons, abbreviations).

Don't use when: information is essential (put it in the visible label) or content is a paragraph (use Overlay/AtlDialog or a popover).

API surface:
- Variant `position`: above | below | left | right → AtlTooltipSpec.atlTooltipPosition
- Text `Content`: tooltip text → AtlTooltipSpec.atlTooltip
- (code-only: `disabled`, `showDelay`, `hideDelay`)

A11y: tooltip role, anchored via aria-describedby on the trigger. Mobile users have no hover, so don't put critical info here.

components.name: Feedback/AtlAlert
components.description:
Inline notification with intent. Maps to AtlAlertSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: a persistent message conveys status that the user should see while continuing on the page (form errors, banner notices). For transient feedback use Overlay/AtlToast.

API surface:
- Variant `variant`: info | success | warning | danger → AtlAlertSpec.variant
- Boolean `dismissible` → AtlAlertSpec.dismissible
- Text `Message`: alert content

A11y: role="alert" for danger/warning (interrupts assistive tech); role="status" otherwise.

Status: 2026-04-27 — `dismissible` moved from Variant axis (false/true) to Boolean Property matching spec.

components.name: Form/AtlCheckbox
components.description:
Boolean form control for opt-in selections. Maps to AtlCheckboxSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: user opts in to one or more independent options (Accept terms, Subscribe to newsletter, parent rows in a tree). Indeterminate state represents a partially-selected parent of mixed children.

Don't use when: choice is mutually exclusive (use Form/AtlRadio + Form/AtlRadioGroup) or binary settings toggle (use Form/AtlToggle — feels more like a switch than a form input).

API surface (Figma → spec):
- Variant `selection`: unchecked | checked | indeterminate — maps to `checked` + `indeterminate` Booleans
- Variant `state`: default | hover | focus — interaction state
- Boolean `disabled`: maps to AtlFormFieldSpec.disabled
- Boolean `invalid`: maps to AtlFormFieldSpec.invalid
- Boolean `required`: maps to AtlFormFieldSpec.required
- Text `Label`: visible label

A11y: native HTML <input type="checkbox">. Indeterminate set via JS property (not HTML attribute). Disabled state uses aria-disabled.

Status: structural restructure 2026-04-27 — single `state` enum split into orthogonal `selection` (unchecked/checked/indeterminate) + `state` (default/hover/focus) Variant Properties; disabled moved to Boolean.

components.name: Form/AtlToggle
components.description:
On/off switch control. Maps to AtlToggleSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: a binary setting takes effect immediately (notifications on/off, dark mode, sync). Visually distinct from Checkbox — the switch metaphor implies "this changes a setting now", not "this opts into a form value on submit".

Don't use when: the binary is part of a form submission (use Form/AtlCheckbox), the setting requires a confirmation step (use Form/AtlCheckbox plus Action/AtlButton), or the choice is one-of-three (use Form/AtlRadioGroup).

API surface (Figma → spec):
- Variant `selection`: off | on — maps to AtlToggleSpec.checked (false/true)
- Variant `state`: default | hover | focus — interaction state
- Boolean `disabled`: maps to AtlFormFieldSpec.disabled
- Text `Label`: visible label

A11y: native HTML <input type="checkbox"> with role="switch" wrapper. The label should describe the setting being toggled, not "Off/On". Disabled state uses aria-disabled.

Status: structural restructure 2026-04-27 — collapsed Cartesian (`disabled-off`/`disabled-on`) into orthogonal `selection` Variant + `disabled` Boolean. Old `state` enum (off/on/disabled-off/disabled-on/focus/hover/active/loading) replaced.

components.name: Form/AtlTextarea
components.description:
Multi-line text input. Maps to AtlTextareaSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: user enters a paragraph or block of text (comment, description, message body). For single-line use Form/AtlInput.

Don't use when: input is a small fixed-shape value (use Form/AtlInput) or a one-of-many choice (use Form/AtlSelect).

API surface (Figma → spec):
- Variant `state`: default | hover | focus | invalid | filled
- Boolean `disabled`: maps to AtlFormFieldSpec.disabled
- Boolean `readonly`: maps to AtlFormFieldSpec.readonly
- Boolean `required`: maps to AtlFormFieldSpec.required
- Boolean `autoResize`: maps to AtlTextareaSpec.autoResize — grow to fit content
- Text `Placeholder`: maps to placeholder

A11y: native HTML <textarea>. Disabled state uses aria-disabled (not HTML disabled) so the element remains focusable and is announced by screen readers — pair with helper text or aria-describedby explaining why.

Status: structural restructure 2026-04-27 — old `state` enum collapsed; orthogonal Booleans now match AtlFormFieldSpec.

components.name: Form/AtlRadioGroup
components.description:
Mutually exclusive option selector. Maps to AtlRadioGroupSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: user picks exactly one of 2–7 visible options where seeing every option is valuable. Wrap Form/AtlRadio children inside the group — the group manages keyboard navigation (arrow keys move selection) and value binding.

Don't use when: option count exceeds ~7 (use Form/AtlSelect or Form/AtlCombobox), choices are independent (use Form/AtlCheckbox), or the choice is binary (use Form/AtlToggle).

API surface (Figma → spec):
- Variant `selection`: unselected | selected — group-level selection state for the gallery
- Variant `state`: default | hover | focus — interaction state
- Boolean `disabled`: maps to AtlFormFieldSpec.disabled — disables every contained Radio
- (code-only: `value: string` — the selected radio's `radioValue`)

A11y: ARIA radiogroup pattern with roving tabindex. Arrow keys move selection inside the group. Wrap in a fieldset + legend if the group has a visible label.

Status: structural restructure 2026-04-27 — single `state` enum split into orthogonal `selection` (unselected/selected) + `state` (default/hover/focus); disabled moved to Boolean.

components.name: Form/AtlRadio
components.description:
Individual radio button. Maps to AtlRadioSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: a single option among a mutually-exclusive set. Always wrap in Form/AtlRadioGroup — the group manages keyboard navigation, focus, and value binding.

Don't use when: option is independent (use Form/AtlCheckbox), the choice is binary (use Form/AtlToggle), or the option set is long enough to need search (use Form/AtlCombobox).

API surface (Figma → spec):
- Variant `selection`: unchecked | checked
- Variant `state`: default | hover | focus
- Boolean `disabled`: maps to AtlRadioSpec.disabled
- Boolean `invalid`: maps to AtlFormFieldSpec.invalid
- (code-only: `radioValue: string`)

A11y: native HTML <input type="radio"> participating in a roving-tabindex group via the parent AtlRadioGroup.

Status: structural restructure 2026-04-27 — single `state` enum split into orthogonal `selection` (unchecked/checked) + `state` (default/hover/focus); disabled/invalid moved to Booleans.

components.name: Display/AtlSkeleton
components.description:
Loading placeholder. Maps to AtlSkeletonSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: content is being fetched and a layout-stable placeholder prevents layout shift on load.

Don't use when: load is <300ms (no perceived benefit) or the loading state is decorative — use Display/AtlProgress.

API surface:
- Variant `variant`: text | circular | rectangular → AtlSkeletonSpec.variant
- (code-only: `width`, `height`, `animated`)

A11y: hidden from screen readers via aria-hidden — assistive tech announces the loading state via the parent region's aria-busy.

components.name: Display/AtlProgress
components.description:
Linear progress indicator. Maps to AtlProgressSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: an ongoing operation has known or unknown duration. Determinate (with value) shows progress; indeterminate just signals activity.

Don't use when: state isn't quantifiable nor ongoing — use Display/AtlSkeleton or a transient Overlay/AtlToast.

API surface:
- Variant `variant`: default | success | warning | danger → AtlProgressSpec.variant
- Variant `size`: sm | md | lg → AtlProgressSpec.size
- Boolean `indeterminate` → AtlProgressSpec.indeterminate
- (code-only: `value`, `max`, `label` — required aria-label for screen readers)

A11y: `label` prop is required by ARIA when no visible label is nearby; otherwise screen readers announce only "progress bar 47%".

components.name: Display/AtlAvatar
components.description:
Avatar with graceful image → initials → icon fallback. Maps to AtlAvatarSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: representing a user or entity. For multiple stacked avatars, use AtlAvatarGroup (deferred — currently illustrated by sibling frames in this section).

API surface:
- Variant `shape`: circle | square → AtlAvatarSpec.shape
- Variant `size`: xs | sm | md | lg | xl → AtlAvatarSpec.size
- Text `Initials`: fallback text shown when no image available
- (code-only: `src`, `alt`, `name`, `status`)

Status: spec field `status` (online/offline/away/busy) is illustrated alongside but not yet a Variant axis — follow-up.

components.name: Overlay/AtlToast
components.description:
Transient notification. Maps to AtlToastVariant + AtlToastOptions + AtlToastContainerPosition in libs/spec.

Use when: non-blocking feedback for an async action (saved, copied, error). For critical interruptions use Overlay/AtlDialog.

API surface (Figma → spec):
- Variant `variant`: default | success | warning | danger | info → AtlToastVariant
- Variant `position`: top-right | top-center | bottom-right | bottom-center → AtlToastContainerPosition (where the toast container anchors on the page)
- Boolean `dismissible` → AtlToastOptions.dismissible
- Text `Message`: notification content
- (code-only: `duration: number` → AtlToastOptions.duration)

A11y: aria-live="polite" for default/success/info; aria-live="assertive" for warning/danger.

Status: 2026-04-27 — `position` Variant axis added per AtlToastContainerPosition. Asymmetric matrix: 5 variants × top-right + 3 illustrative (default × top-center, bottom-right, bottom-center). Cross-product (5×4) can be filled later. `dismissible` Boolean added.

components.name: Navigation/AtlBreadcrumbs
components.description:
Hierarchical navigation trail. Maps to AtlBreadcrumbsSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: pages live in a hierarchy and the user benefits from seeing where they are. For top-level navigation use Navigation/AtlTabGroup or a sidebar.

API surface:
- Variant `items`: 3 | 4 | 5 — illustrative item-count Variants
- (code-only: `separator` string)

A11y: aria-label="Breadcrumb" on the nav element; current page marked aria-current="page".

components.name: Navigation/AtlPagination
components.description:
Page navigator for paged data. Maps to AtlPaginationSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: data spans many pages and user navigates incrementally. For infinite scroll surfaces, omit pagination entirely.

API surface:
- Variant `page`: first | middle | last — illustrative position
- (code-only: `page`, `pageCount`, `siblingCount`, `showFirstLast`)

A11y: nav with aria-label="Pagination"; current page marked aria-current="page".

components.name: Overlay/AtlDrawer
components.description:
Side panel. Maps to AtlDrawerSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: content needs more room than a Tooltip but should not block the underlying view (filters, settings, multi-section content).

API surface (Figma → spec):
- Variant `position`: right | left | top | bottom → AtlDrawerSpec.position
- Variant `size`: sm | md | lg | full → AtlDrawerSpec.size
- Boolean `closeOnBackdrop` → AtlDrawerSpec.closeOnBackdrop
- (code-only: `open: boolean`)

A11y: ARIA dialog with focus trap; aria-labelledby names the drawer's heading. Esc closes. First focusable element receives focus on open.

Status: 2026-04-27 — `size` Variant filled out per spec. Asymmetric matrix: 4 positions on `md` + 3 sizes (sm/lg/full) on `right`. Cross-product (4×4) can be filled later by cloning.

components.name: Display/AtlTable
components.description:
Tabular data display. Maps to AtlTableSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: rows of comparable data need scanning by column. For lists of dissimilar items use Display/AtlCard stacks.

API surface (Figma → spec):
- Variant `variant`: default | striped | bordered → AtlTableSpec.variant
- Variant `size`: sm | md | lg → AtlTableSpec.size
- Variant `state`: default — interaction state (legacy mash-up removed; specific states moved to Booleans)
- Boolean `sortable` → AtlTableSpec.sortable
- Boolean `selectable` → AtlTableSpec.selectable
- Boolean `stickyHeader` → AtlTableSpec.stickyHeader
- Boolean `disabled` → AtlFormFieldSpec.disabled (whole-table disabled)
- Boolean `loading` → row skeletons / spinner overlay
- Boolean `empty` → empty-state placeholder rendered instead of rows
- Boolean `error` → error banner / row replacement

A11y: aria-label required for screen-reader region announcement. Wrapper is keyboard-focusable (tabindex=0) for horizontal scroll.

Status: 2026-04-27 — `state` Variant slimmed from {default,empty,focus,hover,active,loading,disabled,error} to default-only; visual states moved to orthogonal Booleans matching AtlFormFieldSpec patterns used across Form/*.

components.name: Display/AtlCodeBlock
components.description:
Code snippet display. Not in libs/spec — UI helper component for rendering source code.

Use when: showing source code, command-line examples, or other technical content with monospace formatting.

API surface:
- Variant `variant`: default | with-filename | with-line-numbers | no-copy

A11y: <pre><code> with role="region" + aria-label naming the language.

components.name: Form/AtlCombobox
components.description:
Combobox — searchable select. Maps to AtlComboboxSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: user picks one value from a long or dynamic list (50+ options, fetched data, type-ahead). For short fixed lists use Form/AtlSelect.

Don't use when: list is short and known up-front (use Form/AtlSelect), or user must compare options side by side (use Form/AtlRadioGroup).

API surface (Figma → spec):
- Variant `state`: default | hover | focus | open | filtered | selected
- Boolean `disabled`: maps to AtlFormFieldSpec.disabled
- Boolean `invalid`: maps to AtlFormFieldSpec.invalid
- Boolean `required`: maps to AtlFormFieldSpec.required
- (code-only: `options: AtlComboboxOption[]`, `placeholder`)

A11y: combines a text input with a dropdown list. Arrow keys navigate, Enter selects, Escape closes. Disabled state uses aria-disabled.

Status: structural restructure 2026-04-27 — `state` enum slimmed to interaction + open + filtered + selected; disabled/invalid/required moved to Booleans matching spec.

components.name: Navigation/AtlStepper
components.description:
Sequential progress through a multi-step flow. Maps to AtlStepperSpec + AtlStepSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: a task is broken into clear sequential steps where progress matters (checkout, onboarding, multi-page form).

Don't use when: steps are independent (use Navigation/AtlTabGroup) or step count is dynamic.

API surface (Figma → spec):
- Variant `orientation`: horizontal | vertical → AtlStepperSpec.orientation
- Variant `state`: default | completed | error | optional → maps to AtlStepSpec flags (completed/error/optional)
- Boolean `linear` → AtlStepperSpec.linear (when true, only the active and completed steps are clickable)
- (code-only: `activeStep: number`)

A11y: ol with aria-label naming the flow; current step aria-current="step". Each step's clickability reflects `linear` + completed state.

Status: 2026-04-27 — single `variant` axis (default|completed|error|optional|vertical) split into orthogonal `orientation` + `state` Variant Properties. Full 2×4 matrix populated (8 variants total). Vertical-state visual indicators (V/completed, V/error, V/optional) currently inherit V/default content as placeholder — designer polish deferred. Horizontal-state variants carry the canonical state visuals.

components.name: Display/AtlAvatarGroup
components.description:
Stacked avatar group with overflow indicator. Maps to AtlAvatarGroupSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: representing a small set of users (a team, collaborators on a doc, attendees) where individual identity matters less than the count. For a single user use Display/AtlAvatar.

Don't use when: each user needs to be addressable (use a list of Display/AtlAvatar) or the group has more than ~7 members worth showing (use a count + link).

API surface (Figma → spec):
- Variant `size`: xs | sm | md | lg | xl → AtlAvatarGroupSpec.size — mirrors AtlAvatar's size axis
- Text `max`: maximum visible avatars before overflow (default "3") → AtlAvatarGroupSpec.max

Visual: HORIZONTAL auto-layout with negative item spacing for overlap. The trailing "+N" frame is the overflow indicator. Item spacing scales with size (-6 at xs through -12 at xl).

A11y: wrap in a list with aria-label naming the group ("Team members"). Each avatar's accessible name should be the user's name; the overflow indicator should have aria-label like "and 2 more".

Status: 2026-04-27 — promoted from standalone Component to Component Set with `size` Variant axis matching AtlAvatarGroupSpec.size. 5 variants total (xs/sm/md/lg/xl).

components.name: AI/AtlChat
components.description:
Conversational AI surface. Maps to AtlChatSpec in libs/spec.

Use when: a model-driven interaction needs a UI surface. Pick the variant by attention demand: `drawer` for a side panel that coexists with the page, `popup` for a transient floating bubble, `inline` for embedded chat that lives in the page flow.

Don't use when: the user is filling a structured form (use Form/* components) or initiating a single action (use Action/AtlButton).

API surface (Figma → spec):
- Variant `variant`: drawer | popup | inline → AtlChatSpec.variant
- Boolean `open` → AtlChatSpec.open
- (code-only: `status: idle | streaming | error`, message handling)

A11y: ARIA dialog (drawer/popup) or region (inline). aria-live="polite" on the message log so streamed assistant tokens announce as they arrive. Status changes (streaming → idle/error) announced via aria-live region.

Status: 2026-04-27 — merged from three separate Sections (AtlChat / drawer, AtlChat / popup, AtlChat / inline) into one Component Set with `variant` Variant Property matching AtlChatSpec. Old `status` (idle/streaming/error) and `empty` mockup frames remain illustrated as sibling frames in the AI section pending follow-up Variant axis.
